reviews.author: Joyce Tan
reviews.text: Large complex with very beautiful and well renovated/ maintained palaces. You need more that a whole day to explore all buildings. Beautiful flowers are planted in the gardens too. Absolutely well worth the visit!

reviews.author: Chris Taylor
reviews.text: Definitely a must do in Hue. Thoroughly enjoyed our visit. We only planned to stay a few hours and do the short blue route, but just loved wandering around using the audio guide (totally worth the money). Stayed about 4.5 hours in all. Also loved the gardens near the exit.

reviews.author: Martin B
reviews.text: The Imperial City in Hue is an impressive historical site connected to Vietnam’s former imperial dynasties and a great place to learn about local culture and traditions. The complex is large, so comfortable shoes are recommended. It’s best to visit early in the morning, as it gets very hot and crowded later. Also, check the exact entrance location in advance, since Google Maps may sometimes guide you to one of the exits, which can mean extra walking. Tickets can be purchased at the entrance. You can choose a basic entry ticket or a package that also includes visits to other attractions such as the imperial tombs.

reviews.author: Rogan Yeoh
reviews.text: Come before 8am for a much quieter and peaceful experience. Some interiors prohibits photography, so do be respectful when doing photography. There are cultural performances at the Royal Theatre at 10am and 3pm.

reviews.author: Matthew
reviews.text: A great peice of history that they display so well, had a great few hours for such a cheap price of entry, a little busy so expect other tourists to be coming but absolutely worth a visit if youre in the city, it was lovely and was fun to see locals also dressed up traditionally when coming too, had a great number of buildings to visit and just enjoy walking around, the perks of it being so large is that despite so many coming in, once youre in, if doesn't feel like too much.

reviews.author: johnnysheir
reviews.text: Impressive location with interesting historical buildings. Currently there ra lot of renovations so after some time it'll be even more beautiful. I took many pictures. Of course the place is very crowded. Good idea to come in a traditional Vietnamese clothing to take pictures. Better visit on weekdays. There's also a cafe in the imperial garden and some gift shops. Nice experience.

reviews.author: Lý Phối Nghi
reviews.text: Highly highly recommend buy tickets in advance. The ticket price for this Imperial palace is 200k but you can buy Combo tickets for this palace with 1-2-3 tombs. The combo tickets are valid for 2 days. Highly recommend have audio guided, there are many stores behind each buildings. Just 100k. I spent 4 hours in there with audio guided, exploring 80% this area.

reviews.author: Sidney Plant wood
reviews.text: It was okay, you need the headphone to appreciate it. At 200 k thought it was a bit over priced. I feel if we got the headphones it would be a different experience. I wouldn’t go back but I would recommend as it is a staple , not just in hue but of Vietnam. The garden was beautiful and well kept. It was extremely peaceful watching the gardeners work. The flag was mental.

reviews.author: Lei Yutian
reviews.text: Beautiful imperial aesthetic, you can easily spend at least 3 hours there as it’s huge! Ticket office only accept cash, atm is not close to the ticket office so make sure to bring cash, otherwise you can buy tickets online on their official website beforehand.

reviews.author: Chloe Dang
reviews.text: I've noticed many foreigners are confused about which type of ticket to buy to get in here? This area is Hue imperial city. You just need to buy a ticket to enter the Hue imperial city. Combo of 3 sites and 4 sites. These are in different locations. If you buy at the ticket counter, payment by cash. Inside this area they have 2 coffee shops. They have convenient electric vehicle service within the Hue Imperial Citadel, with 4-11 seater electric vehicles (priced at around 240,000-300,000 VND/vehicle/45-60 minutes) for internal shuttle services and green electric bicycles (GCOO) at 100,000 VND for 60 minutes. You could rent it on the left side of the Thai Hoa palace (8 legend ), after you got in the main gate. They also have royal costumes for rent. I didn't rent it, so I don't know how much it costs. Wc: yes. Have a great experience 01.2026

reviews.author: Paulina
reviews.text: The Imperial Palace is definitely a must see if you come to Hue! We spend about 4 hours just walking around the big grounds! There are lots of English signs explaining the sights and different artifacts! We definitely underestimated the size! Plan enough time to see everything! The whole place is so pretty with so many interesting details!

reviews.author: Lukas L
reviews.text: A truly breathtaking place and an absolute must-visit in Hue! Walking through the Imperial City feels like stepping back in time to the era of Vietnamese emperors. The architecture, ancient gates, peaceful courtyards, and beautifully restored palaces create an unforgettable atmosphere. You can almost feel the history in every wall and pathway. It’s not just a sightseeing spot – it’s an experience. Take your time, explore slowly, and imagine life here centuries ago. If you’re in Hue, don’t miss it – this place is pure magic. ✨

reviews.author: Toni H
reviews.text: The Imperial City of Huế is truly one of the most impressive historical sites in Vietnam and a must-visit destination when coming to the ancient capital. From the moment you enter the vast complex, you can feel the solemn atmosphere and the deep historical value preserved within its ancient walls. Built in the early 19th century under the Nguyễn Dynasty, the Hoàng thành Huế served as the political, cultural, and administrative center of the dynasty for more than a century. The architecture reflects strong influences from Eastern philosophy and royal geomancy, combined beautifully with traditional Vietnamese design. The complex once housed palaces, temples, courtyards, and royal residences arranged in strict hierarchical order. Walking inside the Imperial City is like stepping back in time. Although some structures were damaged during past conflicts, many important buildings have been carefully restored, allowing visitors to better imagine the grandeur of the former royal court. The wide courtyards, ancient gates, and moss-covered walls create a peaceful yet majestic atmosphere. The area is spacious and can take several hours to explore fully, so comfortable shoes and some water are highly recommended. Early morning or late afternoon is the best time to visit, when the weather is cooler and the lighting is beautiful for photos. If you are interested in Vietnamese history, royal architecture, or cultural heritage, the Imperial City of Huế is absolutely worth visiting. It is not only a historical monument but also a place that preserves the soul and pride of Vietnam’s last feudal dynasty.

reviews.author: Genevieve Mamer
reviews.text: We stopped here on our way between Phong Nha and Hoi An and it was the perfect rest stop. The buildings and gardens were all so beautiful and depending on what time you go, it was incredibly quiet and laid back. Would recommend getting the audio guide since we didn’t and therefore missed out on some information that could’ve been learned. I’d say you should plan to spend 2-4 hours there. Tickets were very easy to get at the gate and cost 200k per person.

reviews.author: Rob
reviews.text: The Imperial City is a beautiful stop. Full of history and different areas to walk around. The old walls, gates, and palaces make you feel like you have stepped back in time. It is easy to walk around, take great photos, and learn about Vietnam’s kings, culture, and daily life from the past. Kids enjoyed it and was a nice morning outings

reviews.author: Phil Burk
reviews.text: The mother-in-law's palace had an incredibly ornate exterior. It was truly beautiful with painted carvings covering the walls. It was also nice to tour the extensive grounds of the imperial residence.

reviews.author: 최진봉
reviews.text: The architecture of the Vietnamese royal palace was amazing. The architecture of the French colonial period at the end of the palace is unique. It was somewhat difficult to find the ticket office, and there were too few signs explaining the route of movement.

reviews.author: Ken Jones
reviews.text: Great history lesson of ancient imperial Vietnam! Had an audio tour and a walking tour. Both will give you insights but I recommend you walk around and get a feel with the audio tour. You will see more at your own leisure. Do t forget to feed the fish in the royal garden.

reviews.author: Yoofie
reviews.text: Very big complex with lots to explore. Lots of bonsai, a few palaces and smaller temples. The architecture is rich and varied. As the site is spacious it doesn't get overly crowded We bought a combo ticket at a tomb so could just go in easily although we didn't see any queues to buy tickets It's quite a chilled out space but there is a lot of walking involved

reviews.author: Nguyen Le (John’s Tour Hue)
reviews.text: I have visited this place countless times, not just as a local but also for my work. It’s a top tourist attraction—an essential stop in any Hue city tour. This site holds a wealth of historical archives from Vietnam's final feudal dynasty and offers immense architectural value. Although some parts were destroyed by time and war, those remains stand as powerful evidence of its historical significance. I always enjoy coming here with my guests. It’s an opportunity for me to share my historical knowledge and help them understand more about Vietnamese architecture, culture, and the people of Hue. It's incredibly peaceful here and definitely worth a visit, especially since the entrance fee is quite reasonable. Follow me for more interesting updates!

reviews.author: David Ribovski
reviews.text: We loved the imperial city visit. Very cool. The explanations are all in english too. Probably better to buy a combo of 4 tickets to all the sites around hue. I wish I knew that. Overall great experiance.

reviews.author: Paul Duce
reviews.text: I enjoyed it! Pro tip: skip the 100k self audio tour; the numbers on the map didn't align with it and it was frustrating. Get a guided tour instead. Give yourself enough time to do it properly.

reviews.author: Moonlight85
reviews.text: The Hue Imperial City, located in central Vietnam, is a historic walled palace complex that served as the capital of unified Vietnam under the Nguyen dynasty starting in 1802. The city was not only the political center but also the cultural and religious hub of the Nguyen dynasty. The Imperial City is known for its vast, ornate architecture, including a moat, ten gates, palaces, temples, gardens, and royal tombs. It was designed as a ceremonial and administrative center, reflecting the grandeur of the Vietnamese monarchy. The most prominent feature is a tall flag tower that stands as a symbol of the city’s historical importance. Today, the Hue Imperial City is recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage Site, attracting visitors who come to admire its golden architecture and explore the rich history and culture embedded in the complex. Visitors can also experience cultural activities such as traditional costume photo shoots, allowing a deeper connection with the royal heritage. The city is situated along the Perfume River, which divides Hue into northern and southern parts, with the Imperial City located on the northern side. Overall, the Hue Imperial City remains a majestic testament to Vietnam’s imperial past and offers a unique window into the country’s history and artistry.

reviews.author: grizz
reviews.text: big attraction but not much to see as some spots are under reconstruction. went there with a local tour guide which was good as he explained quite well on the history.

reviews.author: Jerome Duport
reviews.text: Amazing place to visit. One of my favorite tour of all what I saw in Vietnam. It is worth taking time (and a guide!) to fully enjoy the place.

reviews.author: Roman Wolf
reviews.text: An awe-inspiring journey into the heart of Vietnamese imperial history and art. A majestic, expansive historical site that is great for exploring stunning royal architecture, profound cultural symbolism, and the intricate life of past emperors. Note: The scale is vast and can involve a lot of walking, but every corner reveals a new layer of history. Best for history enthusiasts, architecture lovers, and anyone wanting to understand Vietnam's royal heritage. Personal Details to Remember: · The serene gardens and pools, especially in the concubines' quarters, which were considered the most beautiful part. · The intimate emperor's private cabinet for concentration and poetry. · The omnipresent use of Chinese script on walls and artifacts, highlighting deep cultural ties. · The powerful and artistic representations of dragons on every surface, symbolizing imperial authority. This citadel is not just a collection of buildings; it's a profound narrative of power, culture, and art that leaves a lasting impression

reviews.author: S. Egberink
reviews.text: It is big, it is huge! One of those sights that won’t be the highlight of your trip, but due to fomo you also don’t want to skip it. Therefore: a must when in Hue. Place could use better maintenance.

reviews.author: Ivana Masek
reviews.text: It was fun and inside of the complex we also had a really nice coffee. It was not busy and we walked around at least 4 hours and then rested on the grass in front of the palace, very pleasant.

reviews.author: Jonatan Jonsson
reviews.text: Great general experience! Found an English tour guide outside the palace, who made the visit alot better, för a very cheap price. Would recommend.

reviews.author: Dat Luong
reviews.text: I'm Vietnamese and this was my first time being here. I was so astonished by how impressive the entire place is. The whole place is huge and full of intricately designed buildings. Pictures alone cannot fully describe how magnificent it is. Give it a go whenever you visit Hue!
